name: Thalassemia (Alpha/Beta)
summary: Thalassemias are hereditary hemoglobin disorders involving HBA/HBB variants. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Often manifests in childhood/adolescence; included as monogenic/oligogenic architecture example (educational only).

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS not applicable as primary model for monogenic thalassemia.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Infections can increase physiological stress; context-dependent.
Nutrition status relates to health context; confounding is substantial.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Thalassemia prevalence varies by ancestry/geography due to historical selection.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Variant interpretation can be impacted by underrepresentation in reference datasets.
population_equity.data_gaps: Environmental exposure linkage evidence is limited and setting-specific.

references.title: Thalassemia: molecular genetics and pathophysiology overview (review)
Infection exposure and immune-mediated disease mechanisms: overview (review)
Extreme heat exposure and health outcomes: overview (review)
